exhibition.label: below threshold
exhibition.case: Two-book consensus 54.2% on the 0.5 line — Belgium's highest-floor SOT play on the board; predicted starter, reported fit, no minutes-veto pattern; possession edge vs. USA supports volume. Ref Makhadmeh confirmed, fouls not playable this tournament. (Line briefly dropped off the board earlier this T-24 window and has since returned — market-coverage gap, not a lineup signal.) T-90 lineup check: official confirmed XI not yet posted as of this pull (~93 min pre-kickoff); ESPN and FOX Sports predicted lineups (published today) independently list Trossard in Belgium's starting XI — treat as predicted, not confirmed, until official lineup drops closer to kickoff.
exhibition.layer_notes: Layer 2: inverted-LW-cuts-inside role transfers cleanly from Arsenal; no set-piece bump (De Bruyne owns dead balls). Layer 3: no sub-risk pattern found this tournament, no veto. Layer 4: no heat/altitude factor (Seattle, evening kickoff); Belgium expected to control ball vs. lower-seeded USA, mild lean toward volume holding up. Lineup status (T-90): not yet officially confirmed — outlets report official XIs typically post ~60 min before kickoff, later than this checkpoint; re-verify before treating as final.

exhibition.label: below threshold
exhibition.case: Two-book consensus 53.5% on the 0.5 line — Embolo is Switzerland's focal point and leading scorer, predicted starter, no injury concern; backup (not primary) penalty taker so no set-piece bump. Ref Barton Cisneros confirmed, fouls not playable this tournament.
exhibition.layer_notes: Layer 2: central-striker role transfers cleanly from club profile; Xhaka is primary penalty taker, Embolo backup only — no bump. Layer 3: no sub-risk pattern found this tournament, no veto. Layer 4: Switzerland's compact transition-based style caps team shot volume vs. a possession-heavy Colombia side — mild lean toward the under on teammates' volume props, doesn't touch Embolo's focal-scorer role.
